affordances.name: Installed dependency unwind map
affordances.mechanism: When a redesign, migration, or exit plan treats options as if they start today, require the answer to map the customers, contracts, interfaces, incentives, approvals, training, and data flows created by the existing path before recommending a target state.

use_when: Early choices are constraining current options more than people realize.
A redesign or migration plan treats the inherited estate like a clean scheduling problem.
Options are being compared as if they start today even though the existing path has already narrowed the feasible set.
do_not_use_when: The reset is genuinely cheap and reversible because the installed base is shallow and switching costs are paid down.
Historical explanation is being used as an excuse to avoid redesign, reset, or responsibility.
The answer would treat inherited constraints as destiny instead of pricing how to unwind them.
case_evidence_needed: The historical path, early choice, or inherited architecture shaping the current option set.
The customers, contracts, interfaces, incentives, approvals, and training created by the path.
The unwind, coexistence, or sequencing costs that make the target state harder than it looks.

treatment_requirements.description: Require the answer to map dependencies created by the existing path and price the unwind before comparing target options.
evidence_required: The prior choices or installed path.
The dependencies created by that path.
The proposed target state or exit.
The unwind, sequencing, coexistence, and stakeholder constraints.
good_output_shape: Explains how those dependencies constrain the next move.
States the realistic unwind or sequencing implication.

diagnostic_questions: Which current constraints exist because of the route we already took?
Which customers, contracts, interfaces, incentives, and approvals does the plan underprice?
Are we comparing options as if they start today?
misuse_guards: Do not use path history as an excuse to avoid redesign or responsibility.
Do not force path dependence onto a case where reset is genuinely cheap and reversible.
Do not treat target-state elegance as proof that the inherited path can be unwound cheaply.

source_evidence.source_quote: initial conditions and historical sequential choices significantly shape outcomes
early choices are constraining current options more than people realize
which customers, contracts, interfaces, incentives, and approvals were created by the existing path
options are being compared as if they start today

review_notes.normalization_note: Batch 5 outcome: strong_affordance_record. The source supports an installed-dependency unwind map for migration, redesign, and exit cases.
dropped_material: PR58 promotes old-behavior reproduction as a separate bounded affordance because the source distinguishes behavioral grooves after new intent from operational dependency unwind.
Did not extract expertise formation or false pattern recognition as separate cards.
Did not infer broad historical analysis beyond source-supported operational dependency mapping.
open_questions: Future packet comparison should test whether path-dependence adds distinct sequencing value beside lock-in.

affordances.name: Old behavior reproduction map
affordances.mechanism: When a system keeps reproducing old behavior despite new intent, require the answer to map the habits, schemas, standards, handoffs, tooling, and approval paths that keep the old route alive, then propose a redesign that changes the groove rather than merely arguing for a new goal.

use_when: A team, person, or system keeps reproducing old behavior despite updated goals or stated intent.
Changing course requires more than a better argument because habits, schemas, standards, or handoff paths keep the old route active.
The issue is behavioral or cognitive path reproduction rather than a platform, migration, or installed dependency unwind.
do_not_use_when: The case is mainly an operational migration, platform exit, or inherited dependency stack; use the installed-dependency unwind map instead.
Historical explanation is being used as an excuse to avoid redesign, reset, or responsibility.
The reset is genuinely cheap and reversible because the established grooves are shallow or already disrupted.
case_evidence_needed: The new intent, goal, or stated change.
The old behavior that keeps recurring despite that intent.
The habits, schemas, standards, handoff paths, tools, approvals, or prior beliefs reproducing the behavior.
The redesign, reframing, scaffolding, or practice change that would alter the path.

treatment_requirements.description: Require the answer to explain why new intent is not changing behavior and identify the path-level intervention that changes the recurring groove.
evidence_required: The stated new intent or goal.
The habit, schema, standard, handoff, tool, or approval path preserving it.
The intervention that changes the path reproduction mechanism.
good_output_shape: Names the old behavior being reproduced.
Maps the path mechanism preserving it.
Explains why a better argument is insufficient.
Proposes a specific path redesign or practice change.

diagnostic_questions: What old behavior keeps recurring after the new intent is stated?
Which habit, schema, handoff, tool, approval path, or standard makes the old route easier than the new one?
What would change the path itself rather than just restating the desired target?
misuse_guards: Do not turn history into destiny or an excuse for inaction.
Do not use this card for clean platform-unwind pricing when installed dependencies are the real constraint.
Do not assume better explanation will change behavior if the old path remains easier to execute.

source_evidence.source_quote: established habits, and foundational structures (mental models or organizational processes) which constrain subsequent choices and often resist change
system keeps reproducing old behavior despite new intent
changing course requires more than a better argument
habits, standards, interfaces, or prior investments are locking the system into the current route

review_notes.normalization_note: PR58 targeted enrichment: old-behavior reproduction is a separate receiver transaction from installed-dependency unwind because the treatment is path redesign, not migration pricing.
dropped_material: Did not promote general expertise formation or latticework benefits as standalone runtime affordances.
Kept history-as-excuse and cheap-reset cases as guards.
open_questions: Packet stress should check whether this card stays distinct from status-quo-bias and lock-in by requiring a reproduced behavior plus path mechanism.

absence_records.reason: The source warns against comparing options as if they start today when prior promises, data flows, and process design already constrain the feasible set.

source_evidence.source_quote: options are being compared as if they start today

absence_records.reason: The source rejects using correct diagnosis of inherited constraints as an excuse to avoid redesign, reset, or responsibility.

source_evidence.source_quote: use that history to avoid redesign, reset, or responsibility
Path dependence is strongest when the real driver is inherited architecture, history, or commitment rather than present-day preference alone.
the real obstacle is not choosing the target state but pricing the constraints the current path already created through contracts, interfaces, installed base, approvals, and training

review_notes.normalization_note: Batch 5 source reading found strong reviewed depth for Path Dependence. PR58 adds old-behavior reproduction as a second, source-backed transaction while preserving installed-dependency unwind.
dropped_material: General expertise formation and latticework benefits remain context rather than separate positive runtime affordances.
No runtime, prompt, lane, packet, or user-facing change is implied.
open_questions: Later packet comparison should verify whether this card makes dependency unwind clearer without duplicating lock-in.
